dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, look what I unearthed from out of a certain hollowed stump! Beneath this thick layer of dirt is the Ambrosia bottle you had me stash away, and we're having it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, now? Why not save it for the Crossroads where, for instance, we're not spattered with the remnants of our foes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak for yourself, I use a bow, I'm clean! But yes, right now. Because if we keep waiting for the perfect time, we'll wait forever. More importantly, you achieved your task, and we barely got to celebrate!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And the moon is new. You make persuasive points, so fine, let's have ourselves a bit. What shall we drink to?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: To the Silver Sisters... if I'm to skulk in shadow with several other goddesses, I couldn't have asked for a better pack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew! That was good, and our work hasn't gone anywhere in the meantime. I suppose we should be getting back to it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Has my little brother lived up to his shining reputation so far? At least he did his part. Soon as I told him about you, sure enough he was plenty eager to let all Olympus know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Apollo was very kind. Though, easy for me to say, I'm not his twin. What'd you tell him anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Only the truth! That I ran into the reclusive Witch of the Crossroads while scouting out our foe... and that she introduced me to a secret Titan killer she's been training up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You just didn't tell him how long ago that was. So now they really think this long-lost relative of theirs is going to find Chronos and slay him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, they think you don't stand a chance! But they don't have a lot of options, so... they'll support you anyway, especially since you're family and all. And you know I will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I recognize those blades! Didn't want to let on before, but... now I guess you know. You best be taking care of them!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not to worry. I am, I swear! So, you were the bearer of the Sister Blades yourself? Did you have to give them up because of me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I gave them up because I did what needed to be done. Hecate so likes her little tests. Anyway I'm glad to see old Lim and Oros in good hands.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, how did you already get Athena's blessing way out here? She's practically a world apart and has been too busy to send messages by Boon...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have her Gorgon Amulet. Perhaps it's a bit rude of me but with it, she can drop in on whichever unfortunate foes I happen to be facing at the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, right. She does like her dramatic entrances, and probably doesn't mind giving hints of her power here and there. Well, here's another hint for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You've tried all my latest Boons by now. Got others I've been mulling, although lately nothing seems to stick! Likely you'd rather have something tried-and-true anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From you, Sister, I'll take whatever I can get! A bit of extra precision in my strikes often makes all the difference.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm not just all about precision here, am I? We gods get cast as one thing and that's it. Father Zeus and his thunder, Uncle Poseidon and his water, Apollo and his... hair.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, do you really understand what Charon's moaning about, or is it all an act... like how creatures sometimes make themselves look bigger, as a form of self defense?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Artemis! I can't exactly parse each syllable but Charon has spent a great deal of time around souls of the dead. It's really not that different. You can communicate with beasts!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I guess, it's just... beasts usually don't have too much to say! Except for cats. Anyway, the boatman probably makes better conversation than some gods I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It's strange, isn't it? He's our own grandfather... Chronos, I mean. But, I feel no connection to him at all! I just want him gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Doesn't matter who he is; he's a threat. We're the Silver Sisters. We'll make him gone. For the sake of the realms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Strictly business then, is it? Even still, funny how it falls on us to settle the older generation's scores.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Got all the way down to the House of Hades, so I heard. Must be nice having fated authority over the lowest reaches of the Underworld... unless you're Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He was most certainly annoyed. Wields a scythe that's twice my height, surrounds himself with feral Satyr-worshipers ready to die for him. I'm not surprised it once took both our fathers and more to bring him down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No reason to compare yourself. They're high and mighty lords of the heavens and the Underworld, but they fought Chronos by necessity. You were trained to do precisely this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Got all the way down to the House of Hades, so I heard. Must be nice having fated authority over the lowest reaches of the Underworld... unless you're Chronos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He was most certainly annoyed. Wields a scythe that's twice my height, surrounds himself with feral Satyr-worshipers ready to die for him. I'm not surprised it once took both our fathers and more to bring him down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No reason to compare yourself. They're high and mighty lords of the heavens and the Underworld, but they fought Chronos by necessity. You were trained to do precisely this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You found Chronos... sank your fangs into his throat. Sister Hecate gave me the gist. How'd you do it...? Found some exploitable weakness, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Mostly I just kept evading a lot. He's very powerful, but must have underestimated my ability to resist his tricks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: He won't always underestimate you, though. Must have his vulnerabilities. The old are seldom wise, and often weak, right? He's bloodied... go for the kill.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You're close, aren't you? To putting Chronos down for good. I know you mustn't share the details even with me, but... you'd tell me if I'm wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd tell you, yes. Now then, I hate to make our meetings brief, but I've a certain matter to resolve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then go resolve it, by all means. Tread softly, and strike true, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Those blades of yours doing you any good? Let me know if you need any, erm... pointers...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Sister Blades are almost like having you by my side in every fight. Though, you can shoot a lot farther than I can throw...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, don't be so hard on yourself, I can shoot farther than anybody! Especially Apollo. Well, just keep going for their vitals, and you'll be fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, Sister! Your brother once had us all convinced he was trying to fight his way to Olympus. Now none of us can believe you're trying to do it for real.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've known Hermes about as long as you, and can count the number of favors he's asked of me on one hand. Besides, I could use the change of scenery.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then by all means! What a time, that you're on your way up to the mountain while I'm on my way down to the Underworld. At least we crossed paths!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at you, Sister! Your brother once had us all convinced he was trying to fight his way to Olympus. Now none of us can believe you're trying to do it for real.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hermes wouldn't have asked me to go if it wasn't important. Besides, I could use the change of scenery.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Then by all means! What a time, that you're on your way up to the mountain while I'm on my way down to the Underworld. At least we crossed paths!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Poor wretches, left to linger here. Could be relaxing in Elysium, or Asphodel at least. No wonder they're upset.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos claimed it was an act of magnanimity, unsealing the Underworld gates, letting the dead roam free. This can't possibly be better than when my father ruled.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, at least it means I can set foot in these parts without inciting some huge territorial dispute! Setting aside the fact that we're already in the middle of one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You probably know Eris has been buzzing around Olympus recently? One good lightning bolt from Father Zeus would normally take care of that, but... he's been occupied.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: One good lightning bolt, how about one good arrow? We'd be saved a lot of trouble if Eris were to be plucked from the sky one of these nights.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd gladly volunteer, it's just that Father has everything well under control. Says I'm needed way out here instead. But if I do spot the wings of Strife... they're going down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey you have an animal companion now! Mine would be with me too, except they won't set foot in the Underworld while they still draw breath... except these birds, which do not seem to care.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I suppose they have other excuses for Ephyra? Some companions they are, leaving you to your devices here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! They're just like us for the most part, except quieter usually. You take good care of yours for me, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've been so far from home for so long. Don't you miss being on Olympus? If not now, then... back before the Titan's siege?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean being with my father and stepmother, my perfect twin brother, and all the rest? Just because I'm willing to aid them doesn't mean I'm willing to live with them again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So what do you think of the family? Most of them are stuck on their mountaintop, though... you've seen what they're like.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh they don't seem so bad! Especially things being as they are. But you can't stand any of them...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey I wouldn't go that far! Though, yes, they can get under my skin. They're going to owe us big once all of this is over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It's really true about the Fates, that Chronos somehow got to them? Doesn't want to take any chances anymore. Did the Fates prophesize their own captivity...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How can you be so casual about this? If the Fates themselves couldn't stop Chronos, we won't have an easy time! We're beholden to chance ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So what if we are! You know not even I always hit my mark. What do I do if I miss? I take another shot. The greater the skill, the less is up to chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You bested Sister Hecate! She told me so herself, and with dare-I-say more than a hint of pride. Though, she's intent on testing you still?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She always said I can't beat Chronos if I can't beat her. And so we spar. I think she's gone easy on me, to boost my confidence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I can plainly see she failed. Gone easy? If there's one thing the Witch of the Crossroads hasn't ever done, it's that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Seen Heracles stomping about? As much as I am normally the trusting sort, I don't quite know about that one. He's been on my bad side for a while. Quite rough with animals.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Artemis, you're goddess of the hunt. Animals must be far more afraid of you than him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't you even start with that, Sister. Animals adore me, except when it's their time. But Heracles goes after them whenever he needs. Just watch it with him, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hermes finally got a hold of you, did he? Sounds like things have really taken a turn on Olympus. Glad I'm not there!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Artemis, how can you say that? It's your home. I'm going to see if I can head up there and lend a hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that even possible for you, born of the Underworld and all that? Then again, you're a witch! Must have a remedy for everything!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Those are the Sister Blades... then, that must be the Aspect of the Morrigan. Looks like it suits you pretty well! Maybe we Night-Moon goddesses are all alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean you never got to wield this Aspect yourself? How did you even learn the waking-phrase you shared?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: One too many questions, Melinoë, but no. That Aspect wasn't for me. Maybe some evening you, too, will have the opportunity to pass along some secret knowledge!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I set up Zagreus with his own waking-phrase one time, for a hidden Aspect of a bow called Coronacht, which Stepmother once used. He was pretty good with it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh truly? And what else would you say he did better than me, Artemis? Certainly he had more charm, but could he sprint faster than me, too?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Absolutely, much faster. Anyway, the point being, I'm pleased to have seen each of you grow strong, and done my part for the Infernal and Nocturnal Arms alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Brought the White Antler with you just in case! The stag who gave it up, it was his time. And I vowed to him I'd make it quick. His Shade and I, we still commune every so often!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Artemis, your tales have this remarkable capacity to warm the heart and yet disturb the mind. In case you ever need a calling other than the hunt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If none of this works out, I was thinking songs and music-making? If Apollo can do it, so can I. Though we've not tired of the hunter's life just yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Glad the old Antler there has served you well. Keep pushing that thing and you're liable to bring the stag who gave it up back to life! Or take over for me on the hunt.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I wouldn't want that... just to be hell-bent on repeatedly hitting my foes in their vital regions. And for some reason your White Antler helps!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Some reason? Our bond's the reason, and the Antler's the connection. Just don't get carried away. I take most shots while out of sight, but you are not as shy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why has Olympus sent you all the way out here, anyway? I thought you'd have gone back by now. You're always welcome in the Crossroads!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate it, Sister, and I do have to report back soon enough. As for why I'm all the way out here, mostly to spy on you! Olympus likes to be informed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain that they do. Do you ever slip up with them? By now the other gods must have their suspicions about you and Hermes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Depends on who you're talking about I guess, but for the most part they just think we're young and flighty. But we're the best they've got.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So when you're not aiding me in my repeated failures, what else have you been up to, Artemis? Some special quarry you're after?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: There might be. Olympus has been shaking, for reasons unknown. Sister Selene asked that I look into it. Surely it's nothing, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: A full Moon... Sister Selene's power at its peak. On nights like this, I really feel my immortality. May our combined strength be enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, is it really true Moros himself showed up at your Crossroads because you summoned him? As in, you literally invited Doom...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It doesn't seem the wisest course when you put it that way. Chronos once was brought low by a prophecy of the Three Fates. I felt they might be willing to intervene again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: And they did so by sending you the embodiment of misfortune and unforeseen consequences. Well if that's what the Fates have in store, you're going to need this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You haven't seen Nemesis tonight, have you? Not that she should be anywhere near here, as opposed to keeping watch over the gateways at the Crossroads.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I haven't seen her, no. But I was tracking her! Those footprints are only slightly harder to spot than she is. She's not supposed to be out here, is she...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...That she is not. She's just trying to make herself useful, I get it. And the Fates themselves know that I could probably use the help. Though, how have you two been?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We each need company, we each need solitude, the same old thing. She's frustrated, which can go a couple of ways depending on the night. Anyway, she's a big girl!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey Artemis, if Nemesis and I... if we were closer, would that be all right? I mean with you. Because if not, just say the word. It's probably not going anywhere anyway...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What am I, Sister Hecate? You don't need my permission, not like Nemesis belongs to me, though I've considered trapping her. In all seriousness, good hunting with that one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So how has Nemesis been doing, she all right? Seems to finally be getting farther in her own stubborn attempts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh she's been well, for her. We've been seeing each other more, and... it's good. As good as she can probably permit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That's great! She did seem a little lighter on her feet, but not too light, of course. You'll have to tell me how you got through her defenses sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine mess we made! Not many hiding-places here compared to Erebus. Might be the end of the line for me... I'm a huntress, not a fisher.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fishing is a form of hunting, isn't it? Anyway I appreciate the backup, and understand if you're now at the limit of your range. Olympus probably expects you back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: They'll want to hear from me! About the situation, and about you, too. You know they still take me and Hermes for their spies? Although, we do tell them a lot...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That massive Cyclops Polyphemus... saw him fast asleep the other night! Then you were able to sneak up and incapacitate him, just like that! I stay far downwind of him, myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm tempted to just smile and nod through this one, Artemis, but my dealings with the Cyclops haven't involved much stealth. Though at least I can get past him, at this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: And how do you get through the Rift beyond the docks, you swim? You still like doing things the hard way. The only way!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Any progress yet with your investigation?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Not near as much as I'd like. Here, let me give you a boost. Maybe you'll get somewhere even if I don't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You saw we're up against Prometheus. His whole thing is how he knows what's going to take place... makes him quite an elusive one, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Titan of Foresight, fighting for the Titan of Time... perhaps merely against the gods who punished him. He may have his reasons, but he's in my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I know precisely what you mean. Though if he really knows the future, wouldn't he choose the winning side...? Careful with him, Sister...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: One disadvantage to my being all the way out here is I'm unable to put arrows through Prometheus from such a range. Apollo says he's been a real bother.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's a problem and a threat... even unarmed, and seemingly not even at full strength, for he still bears old wounds. He has more than enough fury to make up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Great, another angry Titan. Well if he's seething all the time, he's liable to make mistakes. It wouldn't be the first time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't truly believe Prometheus can see the future, do you? He probably just has heightened instincts like yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me? Nobody has instincts as heightened as these, Sister! But I've never claimed to know precisely what's to come. Even if I could, I think I'd miss all the suspense.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've likely heard I didn't get there, Artemis. Didn't even come close.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Sister, I have every faith in you, but not even I believed you were going to vanquish Chronos straightaway. Let's be a little realistic here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Clean and efficient, just the way I like it, Sister. Not been too much to report on my side, and it seems like things are well on yours. Let's keep it moving then, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I can often depend on Sister Selene's light to guide me on my hunts. But where you're going, she can't follow, can she? Nor can I.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And Headmistress Hecate is needed at the Crossroads. I alone have the authority to enter the House of Hades. If I can make it there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, what do you mean if? It's a matter of when, as far as I'm concerned. I'm thinking maybe very soon, in fact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I can often depend on Sister Selene's light to guide me on my hunts. But where you're going, she can't follow easily, can she? Nor can I.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sister Selene has her ways, though I know it's difficult for her, and Headmistress is needed at the Crossroads. I alone have the authority to enter the House of Hades. But I'd have never gotten there without you all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Could really use a proper hunting party for this one! Fortunately it's kind of our thing as Silver Sisters to not have to be in the same place at the same time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: There's a particular chill in the air tonight. I almost broke a sweat! Though our foes were partly to blame. I think I know what's going on, Melinoë, but what is going on?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Testament of Night is what. The Fear and Darkness spread... which may not seem to our immediate benefit, but the Nocturnal Arms grow stronger through it; and the realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well then! In shadow, we serve; to shadow, we return. Olympus figured it was Chronos up to something. I had my doubts, but... wanted to be sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I had more trouble getting here than usual. The Testament of Night again? Nyx herself would surely be impressed with all you've done, spreading fear, creating nightmares...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Through fear comes respect and obedience. Once we're finished, then perhaps the next would-be usurpers with designs on either of our realms are going to think twice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That was a rather good impression of your father, Sister. Well, let's go terrify our foes some more, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: When I became one of the Silver Sisters, I figured all we'd really do was stalk in shadows, make occasional reports... but this is a full-on worst-case scenario!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You sound excited rather than dismayed. You must have known it'd come to this eventually. Not like Headmistress would band us together just for ceremony's sake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No, I guess you're right. Still, we've been together biding our time for so long... I got to thinking we might never get the opportunity to strike. Yet here we are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good being in the Crossroads earlier. My aim is steadier, nerves calm again. I don't get any real peace at home... but it's there to be found in the woods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No place to practice singing at home either, I expect. Is it truly that concerning if Apollo hears? He can't be that much better! And he'd probably be proud of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Or he'd tease me incessantly... I'd likely have to put an arrow through him and get into a heap of trouble. Not prepared to take that sort of risk, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good being in the Crossroads earlier. My aim is steadier, nerves calm again. I don't get any real peace at home... but it's there to be found in the woods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could always do some singing back at home, though, right? You're at least as good as Apollo at this point. He'd welcome your accompaniment at the Palace, I'm sure!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Or he'd tease me incessantly... I'd likely have to put an arrow through him and get into a heap of trouble. Not prepared to take that sort of risk, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You made it quite a ways up on the surface recently, Sister Selene said. Did it live up to all your hopes and dreams?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I tend to have a lot of nightmares, and it did live up to those. The dead are left to linger there. They should be safely in the Underworld!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Chronos evidently has other ideas for how things should be governed. I know your main objective is below, though... thank you for any aid you can provide up top.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So much for visiting the surface, huh? Not seen a trace of you up there! Don't feel bad. You have nice woods here, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not supposed to be up there... could hardly breathe. But there might be something I can do. Hermes wouldn't have asked me to go if it wasn't important.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No, I guess not. I'd offer to escort you though Olympus wants me to stay out of sight. And I won't bother saying not to push yourself too hard...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We're quite the team, aren't we? And that's without Sisters Hecate and Selene backing us up. Shame that we all have to spread out most of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We do have several realms to watch. But yes, I have to say it's been a pleasure working with you recently. Singing with you. Having your friendship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't get sentimental on me, Sister. This is business. And though the world we live in may be looking a bit bleak, at least business is good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen each other in how many nights, Sister? Eugh, I know that's just the way it goes. You're busy, I'm busy, bunch of time goes by, and... here we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Doubtless we've crossed paths repeatedly, just not quite close enough. I feel like a lot's transpired since last we met like this. Where to even begin?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh no need, we haven't seen each other but I've still been keeping up. Olympus knows of all your latest exploits! The ones you wouldn't mind me sharing, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We've bigger problems on Olympus than we counted on, I hear. Our worst fears realized and so on? You'd think I'd be more capable of tracking prey that's Typhon's size...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You were tracking Chronos... me, perhaps. But if it's any consolation, finding Typhon ought to be quite easy at this point! Bringing him down... less so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll take your word for it. My father's much too proud to call me back to aid in our defense. Says I'm still needed here. If he wants Typhon to himself so badly, he can have him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus managed to take Typhon down...? I figure with your aid, probably Sister Selene's too. I guess I wasn't needed up there after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We drove the monster back, but I don't think we've properly discouraged him. Not yet, at least. Though I've a few ideas...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: If your ideas involve an arrow in his eye, of which I hear he has a lot, let me know... moonlight guide you home tonight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I meant to thank you, since our woods and mountains are no longer likely to be crushed beneath a monster's foot or slithering appendages. That's been a big relief.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, anytime. I wouldn't have liked being crushed that way, either. Sounds like Lord Zeus and Queen Hera had some contingency in mind that was best avoided.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Our woods and mountains would have been in even worse shape then. Your way shook Father and Stepmother up a bit... the power to destroy immortals and all that? But it's best they respect you. That's how you get them to be nice!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I hate to even ask, but... have you picked up any trace of Zagreus, by any chance, while in your father's House?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Artemis, it's... hard for me to say. My brother isn't there, but... I sense his presence; our connection. He's not gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I guess that's good enough for me, for now. Although it's much more cryptic than I'd like! Well, wherever he is... he better be all right. And not alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Take this, Sister, and go find your mark.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Tread ever softly and strike swift and true, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Happened to be in the area again, so thought I'd stop on by.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: May Moonlight guide you to your prey, and expose his every weakness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Another night, another duty for the Silver Sisters.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to say, these woods are not my favorite...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't venture any further below, though I can give you this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The skilled huntress keeps her bag of tricks full and with her at all times.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Steady yourself, Sister. You have my full support and more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: May your aim ever be true. Not unlike mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I still think you should try a bow again sometime...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We hunt alone. Although these run-ins aren't so bad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure working with you, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Go cut that wretched Titan down to size for me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: May none dare stand between a witch and her prey.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine night for hunting Titans, don't you think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Picked up the scent of a few stragglers I'll take care of after this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll find your mark. I know you will.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Yours is the most dangerous prey of all. I'm a bit envious!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's a little something to go with those witching arts of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I've got your back, Sister, so just keep moving forward.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We're the Silver Sisters, and we never miss our mark.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We are the Silver Sisters, and this is our time to shine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to say it's nice to get away from it all like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Fancy running into you again, and here of all places!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You tracked me down this time, I'll have you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You were born to do this, Sister, and you're going to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure beats being back on that mountaintop, let me tell you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Moon is full again. May our combined might take you far.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: A new moon marks a new beginning, so take heart, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Moon's light wanes again, but your strength needn't falter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: As the light of the waxing Moon intensifies, so too does our potential, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good hunting on the way up to the mountain, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Father better have expressed his gratitude for all you've done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good of you to deal with Typhon so that we don't have to anymore!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll cover your tracks, but first, here's something for the road.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Next time leave more of them for me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Clean and efficient, just the way I like it, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Here, a little extra Moon magick for all you've done, and still intend to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It's been too long, Sister. Look at you, on your great hunt! Not ideal circumstances but you've finally begun. Know that the family has your back, surprised as they are about this.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No shortage lately of surprises on Olympus or below. It's good to see you, Artemis. But, I must be off. Time's against us, and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Time's an old fool too busy besieging the mountain of the gods to sense the threat headed his way. Go get him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's been a pleasure to have worked more closely with you, Sister Artemis. This is hardly worth the gift of impeccable precision, though I wanted you to have it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Doing the old traditions now, are we? Well sure, then I accept your offering, Sister Melinoë. And I've an offering for you, in kind!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You do know we have barrels of this stuff up on Olympus, right? Used to, at least. Damn near forgotten what it tastes like, come to think...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: May the good memories come flooding back! Whenever you have the time for it, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: What's all this extra generosity from you lately? It's putting me on edge. Don't you go acting like this is goodbye...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's merely that the arrow-riddled remnants of my foes give me occasion to reflect! I tell myself never to take my dear friends for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't exactly drink this on the hunt, you know! But I can squirrel it away for safekeeping. Maybe once all this is settled, we can share a bottle and get properly caught up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Or, failing that, we could always keep on teaming against vengeful spirits and the like! Although it's nice to keep the possibility in mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, thank you! Olympus started running a little low on this stuff. I'll be sure to lord it over the others, as long as you don't mind me exploiting your good graces.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't recall anything about exploiting one another's good graces in the bylaws of the Silver Sisters, so you should be fine! May you enjoy it either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Hard making friends with goddesses, but I have what I need. We can be on our own without feeling alone, you know? I hope you know. That's how it is for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I do know, and feel much the same. When we're not watching one another's backs, we have Selene and Headmistress watching over us. Still, it's always good to see you face-to-face.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You, too. Most of my family I can do without most of the time. You're an exception! We may not be sisters by blood, but... you'll always be Sister Melinoë to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, thought I'd get you some of the good stuff. I suppose there's a real possibility it's become somewhat easier to come by here than where you call home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Ambrosia! Callisto always used to love this stuff. Maybe I'll call on her again once things cool off a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your assignment must have taken you away from her... I'm sorry, I didn't even consider that till just now. She isn't angry with you, is she?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well when she turns into a bear it usually means she is, and she'd been doing that a lot. So it's good we're on a little break! It means I'm much less likely to get mauled.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got something for you and perhaps Callisto here, if you decide to brave the dangers of approaching her again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, thank you very much! I haven't yet decided whether to share this stuff with my Nymph friends, when I could just hoard it all for myself...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wouldn't taste any better that way, so you might as well share it or at least pass it on to someone who shall. Not getting on with the Nymphs as much lately?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: They're not keen about me going off to war. It's like I'm not even the goddess of the hunt to them, which is part of the appeal, granted. But, you and I, we have a job to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This one is just for you, Artemis, so don't even think about giving it to your Nymph friends. Not even Callisto, no matter how threatening her bear form may be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: OK, OK, if you insist! But, what am I supposed to do with it? I travel light, and lately haven't often stopped by home.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about you stash it away, under a rock, or in a hollowed stump or somesuch place? And one of these nights, we can enjoy it for ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that is a proper plan, Sister Melinoë. I'll hold you to it though, you realize. How about we wait until the Moon is new... don't want Sister Selene getting the wrong idea.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I did want to hand over one last bottle, and by last I mean until we find ourselves in different circumstances finally. But this one is for your Nymph friends, not you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Have all my tales of Callisto piqued your interest there, Melinoë? Because I'm open to sharing, and I know she is.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No! I mean, that wasn't my intent, it's just... if they've been close with you, and raised your spirits, then I'm grateful for them. They must be worried about you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know why, when I constantly tell them not to. Though thank you, really. And I'll pass this on next chance I get, with your compliments. But first, promise me one thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just gave you multiple Ambrosia bottles and you want a promise, too? What is it, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You can survive on the surface for a while, so... come join us in our woods sometime. Doesn't have to be soon, wait till all this is over. I just think you'd enjoy it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: ...All right. When all this is over, I promise to meet your Nymph friends in your woods, and enjoy it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We Silver Sisters, we're the keepers of the Nocturnal Arms. The blades of Lim and Oros serve Night's will at your command, but there is more to them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're serious? I'm listening. What would you have me do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: At the Silver Pool, invoke the blades with this: I see your threefold shadow with the Phantom Queen. I think you're ready now. It should be good!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see your threefold shadow with the Phantom Queen. A waking-phrase... thank you for the honor, Artemis. Whose Aspect is this, do you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no one in particular, just another threefold goddess of shadow, like Sister Hecate but of a different age and place. I shouldn't say more, you'll see for yourself soon enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It's strange... I work so closely with Sister Selene, but I've never been any good with her Hexes. Not like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And I am woefully incompetent with bow-and-arrow, not like you. It's probably Nemesis ensuring none of us have it all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you're about to get a little closer to that enviable point. Here, a little extra Moon magick for all you've done, and still intend to do. In case you need it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, there you are! I'm late getting back but couldn't bring myself to leave before you were up and about again. How are you feeling?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Considering the Titan of Time almost sliced me in two last night with a scythe twice my size that cuts through the air itself, I could be worse. Thank you for waiting up for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course! I like what you've done to this place. It's like a proper war camp now! With a taverna, hot springs... I ought to stop in more often. Shine on without me for now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Melinoë, I just wrapped a debrief here with Sister Hecate. This place has changed a lot since last I checked!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hi Artemis, welcome! We've had to scramble a bit to make ready for everything. Can I show you around? Have you said hi to Nemesis?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, no, I have to go. Olympus needs me back up top, at least a little while. Maybe next time, all right?

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I hear you. Bye for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, didn't mean to interrupt. I'd not expected this to be a time for song, though neither am I complaining.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor should you be! Hey, since you're here, why not lend me your voice for old time's sake? Let go your burdens for a bit. Not like they're going anywhere.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I see that look and know you must be going through a lot, Melinoë. But don't forget what we decided here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We leave our worries at the threshold to this little clearing, yes... though, don't you ever slip? Catch your mind drifting back to your concerns?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: That's what the song is for! It doesn't make our troubles go away; it's so we can face them as best we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sister Artemis. I really have to talk to you about something of the utmost importance, right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Nice try, Sister Melinoë, but I won't break that easily. Not here! Now are you going to join in or not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I've heard about the latest happenings, Sister, but we'll discuss them out there on the hunt. Not while we're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Who is that Shade across the river over there, with all the parchment stacks? Never looks directly at me but keeps glancing over, as though it isn't obvious.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you must mean the Record Keeper, who's probably just intrigued by your beautiful song. And likely is keeping meticulous track somewhere of each time you sing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it's a good thing I am not self-conscious in the least.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
endLines.text: Hey Record Keeper! This one's for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll admit it, Apollo had a lot to do with this one. But I still like it... and it reminds me of how we all got to soar together through the sky. Join me a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: On a bit of a break, Sister. Join me for a verse or two?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Apollo gets to do the singing on Olympus, I get to do it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: We all have our little rituals to calm our nerves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I always liked it here, and the Shades don't seem to mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Not concerned about a thing, nor should you be. Not here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Your body has recovered, sure. But what about your mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: This is our song of victory; or any occasion, really. Want in?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: When things don't go our way, we sing... if only for a little bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Things did not go well last night, but that's all in the past.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to get back to the hunt here soon, though... not just yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I can't exactly practice like this on Olympus, you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't think too much about it, Sister, just join in a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: No need to use our plain old speaking voices here, Sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: I feel self-conscious singing on Olympus, but not here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: This song is dedicated to all the Shades out there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Each night brings us new possibility, and I will sing to that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Done with singing already...? Well I'm done with chatting!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Apollo's not around to sing the other part, but you sure are!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you look like hell, Sister. Don't have anything to patch you up, but I can help you kill the wretches that did this to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You're barely on your feet, Sister. Let's save our breath and concentrate on getting through the rest of this intact. Far as we can, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry I couldn't keep them all at bay. I try to pack a few recovery supplies, but have to travel light.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, one of these evenings where nothing's gone right. Have to push through to the next! Or turn this one around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Everybody on the mountain's going on about the Fates finally turning up. Father wouldn't even be in charge if not for them, so he must not have liked the thought that they were gone...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But now the Fates have said the influence of gods shall wane to an extent. I can't imagine Lord Uncle Zeus and the rest are too pleased about that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No, but as major prophecies are concerned, it's really not that bad. And besides, it sounds like this new age of theirs might not be for a while, so... we'll worry about it then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The deed is done, or should I say the task is finally complete. But, you're right back to work!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well... call it a habit, or an opportunity to check up on some things. And thank you, Artemis. I won't forget the flight of the Silver Sisters.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Apollo just had to come along, though I won't soon forget it either. Not felt a victory like that in ages. Wait, why are we still fighting?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To destroy every last trace of the threat we faced, apparently. So I still need you around as much as ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You mean we get to keep this up, and I will have as much target practice as I want? Well OK!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Things are better than I would have thought back home. Still odd to see us dealing with the remnants of the siege, but Father Zeus has been a lot less temperamental, finally.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's good to hear, and I'm glad you had the opportunity to be there yourself. So does this mean I won't be seeing you as much out here? They must want you back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, between us, they still want me to keep an eye on you, powerful witch that you are. But, I do have to check in now and again, which I don't mind. They've been glad to see me, and to hear all about you, and... I can say hi to the Nymphs!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Artemis. Is everything all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: So much to talk about, so little time, Sister. I'm needed elsewhere, but I wanted to make sure to send you off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And how's Callisto doing, everything all right with her? Still frolicking around the woods up there, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, she's been fine. Plenty of Nymphs and beasts to keep her company. She knows that my assignment is long-term, and I can't be in touch by Boon. Though you're all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: More or less. Plenty to do around here lately, though I don't get to do it all. But making sure I'm getting my fair share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Artemis_01
dialogueLines.text: You haven't changed a bit. I'll see you, Nem.
